title: Riileb (species)
content:

{{Quote|Who the krazsch are you?|Tere Metallo uses a word of the native Riileb language whilst shocked|<ref name="RwD" />}}
The '''Riilebs''' were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoid]] [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoids]] that were indigenous to the [[Planet/Legends|planet]] of [[Riileb/Legends|Riileb]]. Members of the species were relatively tall, with [[Color/Legends|gray]], knobbly skin and two antennae, which could detect changes in the biorhythms of other organisms. Using their antennae, the species could detect the mood of those around them, which made them good at setting others at ease and so resulted in others perceiving the Riilebs as a likable people. They also possessed four nostrils on a [[Nose/Legends|nose]] that was much flatter than that of a [[Human/Legends|Human]]; two nostrils were for inhaling and two were for exhaling.

The Riileb [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]] was within [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]]; however, after its discovery by [[Nimbanel/Legends|Nimbanese]] [[Scout/Legends|scouts]] working for the [[Hutt/Legends|Hutts]], the Riilebs refused to bow down to the Hutts and serve them. Following [[Hutt-Riileb conflict|several attempted invasions]] of Riileb by the Hutts, the invaders relented and the Riilebs retained their independence, making them one of the few species in Hutt Space to do so. Riileb society was controlled by the individual in possession of a piece of jewelry known as the [[ClanRing]], which traditionally belonged to the eldest member of the [[MotherClan|motherclan]], descendants of a mythical [[The First Riileb|first Riileb]], who was believed to have created the ring. The Riileb [[Tere Metallo]] was an agent of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebellion]] and the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]].

==Biology and appearance==
{{Quote|Do all Riilebs have this natural talent for games of chance?|A [[Unidentified Corellian smuggler|Corellian smuggler]] comments on Tere Metallo's skill at sabacc|<ref name="Pas">{{JournalCite|7|Passages}}</ref>}}
Riilebs were a [[Sentience/Legends|sentient]] [[Species/Legends|species]]<ref name="RwD" /> of tall, lithe,<ref name="Pas" /> [[Insectoid/Legends|insectoid]]<ref name="AE" /> [[Humanoid/Legends|humanoids]], who stood taller on average than [[Human/Legends|Humans]] and just shorter than [[Wookiee/Legends|Wookiees]]<ref name="Pas" /> at between 2.00 and 2.75 [[Meter/Legends|meters]] in height.<ref name="AE" /> Members of the species shared a number of characteristics with Humans, including two [[Limb/Legends|arms]] each ending in a hand with four fingers and a thumb, and two legs, as well as having atleast two [[Gender/Legends|sexes]]: male and female. Riileb possessed a [[Mouth/Legends|mouth]], which contained [[Tooth/Legends|teeth]], and they had a [[Nose/Legends|nose]] and a pair of [[Eye/Legends|eyes]] on the head. [[Hair/Legends|Hair]] grew from the pate,<ref name="RwD" /> although nowhere else on the body.<ref name="AE" /> Unlike Humans, Riilebs possessed a pair of stamen-like antennae atop the skull,<ref name="RwD" /> which were remnants of their insectoid ancestry, and were capable of detecting changes in other beings' biorhythms.<ref name="AE" />

Such detection could read the pulse rate and [[Blood/Legends|blood]] pressure of Humans. The Riileb nose was flatter than a Human's and had four nostrils, two of which were for inhalation and the remaining two for exhalation.<ref name="RwD" /> The species had ear flaps as opposed to [[Ear/Legends|ears]],<ref name="Pas" /> and fingers that were generally more slender than those of a Human.<ref name="RwD" /> Riilebs' skin was knobbly<ref name="AE" /> and [[Color/Legends|gray]] in color, while their eyes could be pink and their hair silver. They possessed a digestive system able to tolerate [[Ale/Legends|ale]]<ref name="RwD" /> and [[Tea/Legends|tea]].<ref name="Pas" />

==Society and culture==
===Behavior===
{{Quote|We have no games like this on Riileb.|Tere Metallo speaks about sabacc|<ref name="Pas" />}}
[[File:Riileb-AE.png|thumb|right|200px|A Riileb]]
As a people, Riilebs were generally calm and collected, although they usually were determined to succeed in any task they undertook. They were tolerant of the cultures of other species, partially due to the Riileb ability to determine the moods of other using their antennae,<ref name="AE" /> including how stressed or nervous an individual was.<ref name="RwD" /> Some Riilebs used this talent to their advantage in games such as [[Sabacc/Legends|sabacc]], although no such games were played on their [[Homeworld/Legends|homeworld]].<ref name="Pas" />

The Riileb ability to sense others' moods also made the species skilled at setting other beings at ease; thus, many saw them as a personable people.<ref name="AE" /> Riilebs spoke a [[Language/Legends|language]] also known as [[Riileb language|Riileb]], but they were capable of speaking [[Galactic Basic Standard/Legends|Galactic Basic Standard]], as well as one of the native languages of the Wookiee species. While speaking Basic, some Riileb reverted back to their native language if shocked or angry, although sometimes for only as little as a single word of their native tongue.<ref name="RwD" />

Some members of the insectoid species frowned when serious, gave a shrill screech when pleased and gulped down a lump in their throat if worried. The concept of [[Marriage/Legends|marriage]] was a part of Riileb culture, and females who were not married traditionally did not grow a full head of hair; instead, they were allowed to have at least a single braid, which some individuals grew up to a meter in length<ref name="RwD" /> and sometimes entwined with a silky, black tie.<ref name="Pas" /> Male Riilebs, meanwhile, always had a full head of hair.<ref name="TCSWE">{{CSWECite|III||Riileb}}</ref> Riilebs wore [[Clothing/Legends|clothing]] similar to many other sentient beings in [[The galaxy/Legends|the galaxy]], including [[Boot/Legends|boots]], pants, and [[shirt]]s.<ref name="AE" /><ref name="AE2">''[[Alien Encounters]]''</ref>

===Insectoid clans===
Riileb society maintained many aspects of its insectoid heritage. Their cities were built on the same islands that their ancestors inhabited, consisting of complex and twisted alleys and passages similar to the labyrinthine mounds the ancients once built. The society was controlled by the individual who held an item known the [[ClanRing]], a piece of jewelry believed to have been crafted by [[The First Riileb|the first Riileb]], a mythical figure among the species. The ring could rightfully be held only by the eldest member of a group named the [[MotherClan|motherclan]], who were believed to be direct descendants of the first Riileb.<ref name="RwD" />

The eldest member of the group received the ring once they reached the age of 30 local Riileb [[Standard year/Legends|years]], which was the equivalent of 21 standard years of age. Political intrigue, deceit, and infighting were common among the motherclan, however, and those further down the line of inheritance often arranged for the heir of the ring to be removed,<ref name="AE" /> with at least one individual being sold into [[Slavery/Legends|enslavement]].<ref name="RwD" />

==History==
The Riilebs evolved from a [[Riileb precursor species|species]] of social [[Insect/Legends|insects]] on the [[Swamp/Legends|swampy]] [[Planet/Legends|planet]] that shared the name [[Riileb/Legends|Riileb]],<ref name="RwD" /> within the [[Riileb system/Legends|Riileb system]].<ref name="EAOC">{{SW|url=news/star-wars-the-essential-atlas-online-companion|text=''Star Wars: The Essential Atlas'' Online Companion|int=Star Wars: The Essential Atlas Online Companion}}</ref> Their ancestors possessed antennae and lived on the few small islands of dry land found on their hot and humid homeworld, where they built complex, maze-like mounds. At some point after the Riileb developed a feudal level of technology,<ref name="AE" /> their [[Star system/Legends|star system]] came to be considered as within the region of the galaxy known as [[Hutt Space/Legends|Hutt Space]], where it was located as early as [[4000 BBY/Legends|4000 BBY]].<ref name="TEA">''[[The Essential Atlas]]''</ref>

[[File:BlueHutt-LoNH.png|thumb|left|300px|The Riilebs remained independent of the Hutt species (pictured).]]
The species had its first contact from off-worlders when a group of [[Nimbanel/Legends|Nimbanese]] [[Scout/Legends|scouts]], serving the regionally dominant [[Hutt/Legends|Hutt]] species, arrived on Riileb. The Nimabanese attempted to persuade the Riilebs to agree to voluntary servitude to the Hutts, but the insectoids rejected the idea. Following the refusal, several [[Hutt-Riileb conflict|attempts]] were made by the Hutts to take control of Riileb by force and enslave its people, but the Hutt forces, which were controlled by a Hutt named [[Velrugha]], were successfully repelled by the natives each time<ref name="AE" /> depsite the fact that the Riilebs possessed little advanced military technology.<ref name="LoNH">''[[Lords of Nal Hutta]]''</ref> The Hutts gave up and began searching for less-resistant worlds to conquer, leaving the Riilebs as one of the few independent species in Hutt Space,<ref name="AE" /> along with the [[Sakiyan/Legends|Sakiyan]] species of [[Sakiya]].<ref name="UAA">''[[Ultimate Alien Anthology]]''</ref>

Following the discovery of the Riilebs by the rest of the galaxy, they gained access to the standard levels of technology available throughout known space, which they implemented on their homeworld. Due to its independence, Riileb became known as a haven within Hutt Space for those wishing to avoid the Hutts. Nonetheless, the planet saw little interstellar traffic, bar those looking to trade in [[heklu]]—amphibious animals native to the planet—which were eaten and considered a delicacy within the [[Core Worlds/Legends|Core Worlds]].<ref name="AE" />

==Riilebs in the galaxy==
===A Riileb betrayed===
{{Quote|The Empire did this.|Tere Metallo speaks of her facial scar|<ref name="Pas" />}}
[[File:Tere_Metallo_SWJ6.jpg|thumb|right|250px|Tere Metallo worked for the Rebel Alliance and the New Republic.]]
Riilebs were not a common sight in the galaxy, although not unknown. Many left their homeworld to become traders or to use their mood detection abilities to excel at careers in [[Bartender/Legends|bartending]], [[psychiatry]], or as [[Doctor/Legends|physicians]].<ref name="AE" />

[[Tere Metallo]] was a female Riileb who was born to the motherclan and stood to inherit leadership at the age of 30. At the age of 25, however, she was sold into enslavement by a jealous [[Tere Merallo's sister|sister]]. Metallo was rescued from enslavement by a [[Corellian/Legends|Corellian]] [[Smuggler/Legends|smuggler]] named [[Bek Nataal]], who taught her a large amount about smuggling and [[Pilot/Legends|piloting]] before he was [[Death/Legends|killed]] during an unexpected inspection by officials of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]].<ref name="RwD" />

The Riileb was captured by the Imperials and imprisoned and [[Torture/Legends|tortured]] before escaping to<ref name="RwD" /> the planet<ref name="TEA" /> [[Corellia/Legends|Corellia]], where she took up work repairing [[Starship/Legends|starships]]. When she earned enough [[Galactic Credit Standard/Legends|credits]], she bought her own ship, a [[Suwantek Systems]] [[TL-1800 transport|TL-1800 freighter]] that she named ''[[Star Quest]]''. For several years, she made legitimate runs in the [[Mid Rim/Legends|Mid Rim]]. During this time, she took on a Human co-pilot named [[Matt Turhaya]].<ref name="RwD" />

===Metallo and the Alliance===
During a stop on the planet [[Kabaira]], the pair aided members of a cell of the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]], as [[Del Hunter|one member]] was an old acquaintance of Metallo.<ref name="Pas" /> The pair then began running supplies for the Rebel Alliance, although Turhaya was killed while doing so. Metallo eventually accepted a commission in [[Alliance Intelligence/Legends|Alliance Intelligence]] after the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] and used her work as a free trader to slip onto Imperial-controlled worlds and provide intelligence from them. After the transition of the Rebel Alliance into the [[New Republic/Legends|New Republic]], Metallo continued working for the organization; in [[9 ABY/Legends|9 ABY]], she took up a [[Mission to Sarahwiee|mission]] to destroy a [[Sarahwiee Imperial base|secret Imperial research facility]] on the planet [[Sarahwiee]].<ref name="RwD" />

She and her crew took a job from Imperials on the planet [[Garos IV]] to ship supplies to the facility, planning to destroy the base with explosives. During their flight to Sarahwiee, Metallo discovered that a member of the resistance against the Imperials on Garos IV had stowed away on their ship in hope of rescuing a friend who was being held on Sarahwiee. The Riileb agreed to transport the Human, [[Alexandra Winger]], to the planet and on the surface helped save her friend, a Human scientist named [[Carl Barzon]]. Once at the facility, the group planted explosives and escaped after a short firefight with Imperial [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]].<ref name="RwD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The Riilebs first appeared in the short story "[[Rendezvous with Destiny]]," authored by [[Charlene Newcomb]], illustrated by [[Mike Vilardi]], and published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 6|sixth issue]] of the ''[[Star Wars Adventure Journal]]'' by [[West End Games]] in [[May]] [[1995]].<ref name="RwD" /> The species were given stats for ''[[Star Wars: The Roleplaying Game]]'' that favored perception and mechanics in [[Brian Smithson]]'s article "[[Alien Encounters (article series)|Alien Encounters]]," which was illustrated by [[Pablo Hidalgo]] and published in the [[Star Wars Adventure Journal 13|thirteenth]] issue of the Adventure Journal in May [[1997]].<ref name="AE" />
